nba.note: Pistons 115, Warriors 101 · Nuggets 121, Raptors 115 — full 6-game Friday slate

nhl.note: Colorado clinches — Makar closing in on 500 career points; Toronto vs Ottawa in Atlantic showdown

wbc.label: MLB — Spring Training
wbc.note: Opening Night 4 days away — March 25 · Rosters finalizing this weekend

topStories.headline: KAT, Brunson bail out the Knicks — 14 straight over the Nets
body: The New York Knicks trailed by 13 in the first half, blew a 14-point fourth-quarter lead, and still found a way. Karl-Anthony Towns finished with 26 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 17, and OG Anunoby added 16 as the Knicks survived Brooklyn 93–92 on Friday to win their 14th consecutive game in the local rivalry — the longest winning streak for either team in the series. Ben Saraf had a chance to win it for the Nets at the buzzer, but his 45-footer was off. Josh Minott had 22 for Brooklyn in a loss that keeps the Nets firmly in lottery territory.

topStories.headline: Rockets end Atlanta's 11-game run — KD and Jabari dominate
body: The Atlanta Hawks' 11-game winning streak — the longest in the East this season — came to a halt Friday night. Kevin Durant scored 25 points and Jabari Smith Jr. added 23 as Houston rolled to a 117–95 victory. The Rockets have quietly crept into serious West playoff seeding contention, and Durant looked every bit the postseason-ready veteran Atlanta had no answer for. The Hawks (now 42–28) still hold a top-four Eastern seed, but the loss exposed some defensive lapses that could matter come April.

topStories.headline: Grant's buzzer three lifts Portland — Timberwolves drop a killer one
body: Jerami Grant hit a go-ahead three-pointer with 22.2 seconds left as the Portland Trail Blazers escaped Minnesota 108–104, handing the Timberwolves a painful late-season loss. Minnesota, fighting for playoff seeding, had led for most of the fourth quarter. The win keeps Portland in the play-in conversation — one of the NBA's most unexpectedly competitive fringe stories of March.

stories.headline: Brown leads Celtics, Pistons keep rolling — Friday's full slate
body: Jaylen Brown was named Player of the Night after leading Boston past Memphis 117–112 in a tighter-than-expected road win. The Celtics (50–19) are the East's runaway 1-seed and maintaining their dominant position. In Detroit, the Pistons (51–18) beat Golden State 115–101 — their Eastern Conference-leading record remains intact even with Cunningham's back still being monitored. Nikola Jokić and the Nuggets handled Toronto 121–115 as Denver holds steady in the West's 4-seed.

stories.headline: Hawks streak ends but their seed is safe — West fringe is chaos
body: Atlanta's 11-game run is over but the Hawks aren't panicking — they're still firmly in the top four of the Eastern Conference. The West fringe from 5 through 9 remains a blur: OKC sits 1st, but seeds 5–9 are separated by just over two games. Portland's win and Minnesota's loss only tighten the scramble. Every game in the West matters now.

stories.headline: Colorado clinches — Makar closing in on 500 career points
body: The Colorado Avalanche became the first team in the NHL to clinch a playoff berth Friday, beating Chicago to lock up a postseason spot with weeks still to go. It's the expected outcome for a club that's been the West's class team all season. Cale Makar is closing in on 500 career points — a milestone that, if he hits it this week, would make him one of the fastest defensemen in history to reach the mark. Colorado's regular season has been a clinic in sustained excellence.

stories.headline: Toronto-Ottawa heats up — four points separate three Atlantic teams
body: The Maple Leafs and Senators met Friday in a division game with real stakes. Toronto (40–22–8) and Ottawa (37–24–9) are separated by just four points in the Atlantic standings with the Bruins lurking. Every Atlantic game is a playoff audition now — these teams are playing meaningful hockey in the season's final three weeks. The winner of the division race likely earns home-ice advantage in the first round.

stories.headline: Four days to Opening Night — rosters must be set by Sunday
body: MLB teams face roster submission deadlines this weekend ahead of Opening Night on March 25. The most watched situations: the Yankees are deciding between two rotation spots with Juan Soto and Gerrit Cole headlining the lineup; the Dodgers are managing a crowded outfield after WBC returns; and the Padres are watching Fernando Tatis Jr.'s wrist closely after his WBC workload. Spring training games wrap Sunday, and the real thing starts in four days.
